D11 ComputationalL2 UnderstandReference

Neural Style Transfer — Gatys 2015 Loss Composition

Optimise input image to match content (deep-feature L2) + style (Gram-matrix of features); slider trades off content fidelity vs stylistic look

Gatys et al. (2015) found that VGG19 feature responses split into "content" (mid layers) and "style" (Gram correlations across layers). Optimising pixels to satisfy both gives the famous "photo painted by Van Gogh" effect. Modern fast NST uses a feed-forward network distilled from this optimisation.

Slide α/(α+β): pure content (left) ↔ pure style (right).
